codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the prior interactive-path blockers. `install` still routes through `interactive(undefined, targets)` (`cli/src/cli.ts:143`), but `interactive` now applies support-matrix filtering before item selection (`cli/src/interactive.ts:270-278`) and `runInstall` now guards execution per category with `checkCategorySupport` before hooks/subagents/sync calls (`cli/src/interactive.ts:150,162,176,183`). This enforces warning+omit behavior in TTY flows and prevents unsupported category execution for selected targets.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is a guidance mismatch between mission-level policy and the worker skill for interactive evidence: the skill mandates manual interactive verification, while mission AGENTS allows deterministic automated evidence + code-path verification when interactive automation is impractical.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/cli-refactor-worker/SKILL.md:27 requires manual interactive/non-TTY verification; mission AGENTS.md:43 explicitly allows deterministic automated tests plus code-path verification as alternative prompt UX evidence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Repo defaults currently have no installable hook scripts, which affects expected output markers in routing tests (hook execution may report informational no-hook message instead of DRY-RUN Hook lines). This quirk is not captured in shared library guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `hooks/` contains only `README.md`; `cli/src/hooks.ts:11` prints 'No hook scripts found (add .sh files to hooks/)'. Worker transcript shows test expectations had to be adjusted around this behavior.

summary: PASS: commit ff8667a adequately fixes the previously reported interactive warning+omit gaps. Original blocking findings for `cli/src/cli.ts` routing and `cli/src/interactive.ts` execution behavior are resolved by new category filtering and per-category support checks, and no blocking issues remain.
